questions.question: Define a structure in C and explain why it is used with an example.
keyPoints: Structure is a user-defined data type that groups related data items of different types
Used when we need to store heterogeneous data (different types) together
Example: Student record with name, rollno, marks
Provides better organization compared to using separate variables

questions.question: Explain the difference between an array and a structure with suitable examples.
keyPoints: Array stores elements of SAME data type; structure stores data of DIFFERENT types
Array elements are accessed by index; structure members are accessed by name
Example array: int marks[5]; Example structure: struct Student { int rollno; float marks; };

questions.question: Write the syntax to define a structure and declare its variables. Explain each component.
keyPoints: Use 'struct' keyword followed by tag name
Member declarations inside curly braces
Variables can be declared at definition time or later
Example: struct Book { char title[30]; int pages; float price; } b1, b2;

questions.question: How do you access structure members? Explain with an example.
keyPoints: Use dot (.) operator for structure variables
Use arrow (->) operator for pointers to structures
Example: s1.rollno = 101; where s1 is structure variable
For pointer: p->rollno is equivalent to (*p).rollno

questions.question: What is structure padding? Why does it occur?
keyPoints: Structure padding adds extra bytes between members for memory alignment
Compiler adds padding to improve access speed
Occurs to ensure members are aligned at their natural boundaries
May cause structure size to be larger than sum of member sizes

questions.question: How is a structure variable initialized at the time of declaration? Give an example.
keyPoints: Use curly braces with values in member order
Example: struct Student s1 = {101, "John", 85.5};
Partial initialization sets remaining members to zero (global) or garbage (local)
All members must be initialized in the order they are declared

questions.question: What is the arrow operator (->) in structures? When is it used?
keyPoints: Arrow operator accesses structure members through a pointer
Syntax: pointerVariable->memberName
Equivalent to dereferencing first: (*pointerVariable).memberName
Used when structure variable is passed by address or dynamically allocated

questions.question: Explain structures in C with the help of a program to store and display information of 5 students (roll number, name, and marks).
keyPoints: Define structure Student with int rollno, char name[50], float marks
Declare array of structures to store 5 students
Use loops to input and output data
Access members using dot operator
Show complete working program with proper input/output

questions.question: Compare arrays and structures. List at least 5 differences and explain when to use each.
keyPoints: Array: same type elements; Structure: different type members
Array: indexed access; Structure: member name access
Array: contiguous memory; Structure: contiguous but with padding
Use array for homogeneous data; Use structure for heterogeneous data
Other differences: initialization, comparison, assignment rules

questions.question: Write a C program to demonstrate the use of typedef with structures. Explain its advantages.
keyPoints: typedef creates an alias for structure type
Eliminates need to write 'struct' keyword repeatedly
Makes code cleaner and more readable
Show example: typedef struct { } Student; then Student s1;
Compare with and without typedef

questions.question: Explain nested structures with a suitable example. Write a program to store date of birth of a person using nested structures.
keyPoints: Structure can contain another structure as member
Define Date structure with day, month, year
Define Person structure with name and Date member
Access nested members: person.dob.day
Show complete program demonstration
